# Agent Folder Template

Templates for the portable agent deployment files that get pushed to GitHub.
These live in `agent-skills/orgs/{org}/agent-templates/{org}-{agent-id}/`.

## .env.example Template

```bash
# Agent: {org}-{agent-id}
# Role: {agent-role-description}
# Generated by Agent Builder on {YYYY-MM-DD}
#
# Fill in the values below and save as .env in the agent workspace,
# or use `/setup {org}-{agent-id}` to configure via chat.
# NEVER commit this file with actual values.

{for each skill assigned to this agent:}
# --- {skill-name} ---
# {skill-goal}
{for each env_var in skill:}
{ENV_VAR_NAME}=           # {description}{if help_url} — {help_url}{endif}
{endfor}

{endfor}
```

### Example .env.example

```bash
# Agent: ruh-support-classifier
# Role: Classify support tickets and route to teams
# Generated by Agent Builder on 2026-03-11
#
# Fill in the values below and save as .env in the agent workspace,
# or use `/setup ruh-support-classifier` to configure via chat.
# NEVER commit this file with actual values.

# --- ruh-zendesk-ingester ---
# Fetch new unclassified tickets from Zendesk
ZENDESK_API_TOKEN=           # Zendesk API token — Admin > API
ZENDESK_URL=                 # Zendesk instance URL (e.g., https://yourcompany.zendesk.com)

# --- ruh-llm-classifier ---
# Classify tickets using LLM
OPENAI_API_KEY=              # OpenAI API key — platform.openai.com/api-keys

# --- ruh-sla-tracker ---
# Track SLA deadlines
SLA_DB_URL=                  # PostgreSQL connection string
```

## openclaw-entries.json Template

```json
{
  "_comment": "Merge this into ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json to register this agent",
  "agent": {
    "id": "{org}-{agent-id}",
    "name": "{org}-{agent-id}",
    "workspace": "{workspace-path}",
    "agentDir": "~/.openclaw/agents/{org}-{agent-id}/agent",
    "model": "{model — default: openai-codex/gpt-5.4}"
  },
  "skills": {
    "{for each skill:}": "---",
    "{org}-{skill-name}": {
      "enabled": true,
      "env": {}
    }
  },
  "bindings": [
    {
      "_comment": "Add channel bindings if this agent handles specific channels",
      "agentId": "{org}-{agent-id}",
      "match": { "channel": "telegram", "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "{group-id}" } }
    }
  ],
  "subagent_config": {
    "_comment": "Add to parent agent if this agent is spawned as sub-agent",
    "allowAgents": ["{org}-{agent-id}"]
  },
  "cron": [
    {
      "_comment": "Add cron entries if this agent has scheduled tasks",
      "schedule": "{cron-expression}",
      "agentId": "{org}-{agent-id}",
      "task": "Run the {skill-name} skill",
      "label": "{short-label}"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Example openclaw-entries.json

```json
{
  "_comment": "Merge into ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json for ruh-support-classifier",
  "agent": {
    "id": "ruh-support-classifier",
    "name": "ruh-support-classifier",
    "workspace": "/home/daytona/workspaces/ruh-support-classifier",
    "agentDir": "~/.openclaw/agents/ruh-support-classifier/agent",
    "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.4"
  },
  "skills": {
    "ruh-zendesk-ingester": { "enabled": true, "env": {} },
    "ruh-llm-classifier": { "enabled": true, "env": {} },
    "ruh-sla-tracker": { "enabled": true, "env": {} }
  },
  "bindings": [],
  "cron": [
    {
      "schedule": "*/5 * * * *",
      "agentId": "ruh-support-classifier",
      "task": "Run the ruh-zendesk-ingester skill",
      "label": "Ticket ingestion"
    }
  ]
}
```

## README.md Template

```markdown
# {org}-{agent-id}

{agent-role-description}

## Skills

{for each skill:}
- **{skill-name}** — {skill-goal}
{endfor}

## Setup

### 1. Register Agent

```bash
openclaw agents add {org}-{agent-id} --workspace {workspace-path} --model openai-codex/gpt-5.4
```

### 2. Configure Credentials

Option A — Via chat:
```
/setup {org}-{agent-id}
```

Option B — Manual:
Copy `.env.example` to `.env`, fill in values, then merge `openclaw-entries.json` into `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.

### 3. Restart Gateway

```bash
openclaw gateway restart
```

## ENV Variables

| Variable | Required | Used By | Description |
|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
{for each env_var across all skills:}
| `{VAR_NAME}` | {yes/no} | {skill-name} | {description} |
{endfor}

## Architecture

- **Type**: {single-skill / multi-skill / multi-agent}
- **Model**: {model}
- **Created**: {date}
- **Org**: {org}
```

## Notes

- `.env.example` and `openclaw-entries.json` are pushed to GitHub (no secrets)
- Actual credentials go in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (local only)
- The workspace itself (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, memory/) is runtime data — created by skill-factory during setup
- `openclaw-entries.json` is a convenience file — the agent-setup skill reads it and merges into openclaw.json
